title: Causal field/time-aware sequential tabular specialist per driver-race
summary: This data is sequential tabular, not iid tabular: `Race`, `Driver`, and `Year` are static context, while tyre wear and pace features evolve lap by lap.
rationale: This data is sequential tabular, not iid tabular: `Race`, `Driver`, and `Year` are static context, while tyre wear and pace features evolve lap by lap. Recent sequence-tabular work explicitly separates static and dynamic fields, which matches this competition better than adding more hand-made thresholds.
implementation_hint: Build a causal sequence model over each `Race x Driver` trajectory, with separate embeddings for static fields and dynamic lap tokens, masking future laps. Restrict context to current stint or last N laps, predict next-lap hazard at every step, and optionally late-fuse the sequence score with the existing GBDT/AutoGluon score.
expected_effect: Should capture stint shape, abrupt cliff onset, and extension behavior that hand-engineered finish-window rules miss, especially for rows near pit-window boundaries.
risk: Overfitting on race/driver ids and unseen drivers is the main failure mode. Use strict grouped folds, shallow capacity, and treat this as a specialist to blend rather than a full replacement.
